title: Include cgroup manager in `podman info` output
body: This is very useful for debugging cgroups v2, especially on rootless - we need to ensure people are correctly using systemd cgroups in these cases.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Sample output:

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
```

This is begging for a test; could you real quickly just add
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/005-info.bats b/test/system/005-info.bats
index 3f1efd364..333dbcf90 100644
--- a/test/system/005-info.bats
+++ b/test/system/005-info.bats
@@ -19,6 +19,8 @@ graphRoot:
 graphStatus:
 imageStore:\\\s\\\+number: 1
 runRoot:
+cgroupManager:
+cgroupVersion: v
 "
     while read expect; do
         is "$output" ".*$expect" "output includes '$expect'"
```
(I will need to fix `parse_table` some day to better handle `|` in expressions, but for now just a simple key-is-present test would be nice)

prior-fedora tests looks unhappy, I'm not sure if it's a flake.
Changes LGTM.
Although if you ever go back into the code, I'd prefer having lines 107 through 116 moved above `info := define.Hostinfo{` at line 87 so that you could also set the `info.CgroupsVersion` within that same definition block.  

Probably too late to change CgroupsVersion to CgroupVersion at this point too?

"podman run two containers with same IP" is flake #7096

"  podman search with wildcards" is new to me, but looks like a registry failure (hopefully transient):
```
error searching registry \"registry.redhat.io\": couldn't search registry \"registry.redhat.io\": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: Please login to the Red Hat Registry using your Customer Portal credentials. Further instructions can be found here: https://access.redhat.com/RegistryAuthentication"
```
I've restarted the failing test.
